# Vivere

> TypeScript framework for Discord bots built on discord.js, with typed feature files, deterministic manifests, Gateway runtime, HTTP interaction support, and test tooling.

## Project

- Repository: https://github.com/jb0xyz/vivere
- Package: `@jb0xyz/vivere`
- License: MIT
- Primary package: [packages/vivere](./packages/vivere)
- Example bot: [examples/basic-bot](./examples/basic-bot)

## Core Model

Vivere projects define bot features as typed files. The common layout is:

- `src/app/vivere.ts`: binds project services with `createVivere<Services>()` and re-exports authoring helpers.
- `src/commands/*.ts`: slash commands and context menu commands.
- `src/events/*.ts`: Discord client events.
- `src/components/*.ts`: buttons, select menus, and modals.
- `vivere.config.ts`: discovery roots, development guild, and plugins.
- `.vivere/manifest.json`: deterministic description of the bot surface.

## Main APIs

- `createVivere<Services>()`: creates project-local helpers with service typing.
- `createApp(...)`: starts the Gateway runtime with explicit definitions or `discover`.
- `defineConfig(...)`: typed `vivere.config.ts`.
- `defineCommand(...)`: slash commands, options, autocomplete, subcommand routes through folders.
- `defineUserCommand(...)` and `defineMessageCommand(...)`: context menu commands.
- `defineEvent(...)`: Discord client events.
- `defineButton(...)`, `defineSelect(...)`, `defineModal(...)`: interactive components with signed params.
- `definePlugin(...)`: reusable bundles of commands, events, and components.
- `defineMiddleware(...)`: before/after/error pipeline.
- `requirePermission(...)`, `requireRole(...)`, `cooldown(...)`, `rateLimit(...)`: declarative policies recorded in the manifest.
- `opt`, `param`, `field`: typed builders for command options, component params, and modal fields.

## Runtime Ports

- Stores: `KeyValueStore` and `RateLimitStore`; memory implementations are exported from `@jb0xyz/vivere`.
- Testing: `@jb0xyz/vivere/testing` exports `createTestBot` for running handlers without Discord.
- HTTP: `@jb0xyz/vivere/http` exports `createHttpHandler` for Discord HTTP interactions.

## Manifest Contract

Run `vivere build` to write `.vivere/manifest.json`. Run `vivere build --check` in CI to detect drift. The manifest is function-free and deterministic; use it to inspect commands, events, components, middleware, and policies without importing the bot.

## CLI

- `vivere build`: generate `.vivere/manifest.json`.
- `vivere build --check`: verify the committed manifest is current.
- `vivere sync`: register commands to the configured development guild.
- `vivere sync --global`: register global commands.
- `vivere dev`: run the project and restart on changes.

## Scaffolding

`@jb0xyz/create-vivere` provides the interactive project creator. It generates localized project files, `AGENTS.md`, and Gateway or HTTP starter code.
